content:
✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: I accept this contribution because it's very exciting to solve it

I might be asking too much from you, but can you make this only fastest and reverse? I just absolutely hate shortest because I have to keep dealing with the occasional low-ranked Ruby coder who always beats me no matter how good my Python3 solution is. Clash of code should be more of a test of speed, and your puzzle definitely fits that category. I understand if anyone disagrees with me, but that’s just how I feel.

It is quite likely that something similar exists, but unless there is exactly the same clash, it shouldn't be a problem. (let's hear from others)
However, I would change a few things to make the statement clearer:

Goal:
* Introduce all the variables here, and don't explain them later. I suppose following:
"Create a rectangle of width [[W]] and height [[H]] using an outer character [[OC]] as a border and an inner character [[IC]] to fill the rectangle."

Input:
* Change line to Line
* Follow number of the line by a colon, e. g. <<Line 1:>>
* If [[W]] and [[H]] are explained in the Goal, I see no need to explain them in Input as well.
* Possibly change "character" to "Character"

Output:
* I think that word order "height [[H]]" flows better than "[[H]] height". The same goes for width.
* For consistency with "a border of", I would change "filled with" to "a fill of".

Constraints:
* The maximum of [[W]] and [[H]] in your test cases is 20. I would also change the constraints to follow with this, or possibly change test cases.
* For readability, add spaces around operators, as follows: "2 < [[W]], [[H]] <= 100"

Test cases:
* For a faster understanding, I would change the first test, shown as an example in the statement, to have easily distinguished characters.


That's just my point of view; if you disagree in some points, just ignore them.

I completely agree and have changed everything you mentioned.
and for the goal i just said width and height will be provided
yeah that makes sense  and ngl dude that aint that big of an ask
You didn't change the typography in Constraints. Operators (< and <=) should be surrounded with space, and a comma should be followed by a space character.
anything else before i make it ready

I would still introduce variables [[W]] and [[H]] in the statement: "Width [[W]] and height [[H]] will be provided." And remove "(width and height of the rectangle)." from the Input section.
Possibly change the tense, "will be provided" → "is provided".

Also, I think that "character" in Lines 2 and 3 should start with an uppercase letter: "Character".

I'm not sure about the goal, regarding consistency of "as a border" and "to fill the rectangle"... But I don't see it as a problem.

Maybe just delete the dot after [[H]] in Line 1. Otherwise, looks good.

Anyways, here's my (unprofessional) try at the shortest code:
```py
p=input
w,h,o,i=*map(int,p().split()),p(),p()
print(o*w,*[o+i*(w-2)+o]*(h-2),o*w,sep="\n")
```

what language is this
if its python it dont look like it but ima see if that works

good news: it works
bad news: i dont know how it works
nice job tho
